title: The Ten-Minute Reclaim
subtitle: A short evening ritual for taking back a piece of the day for yourself
excerpt: A brief evening ritual for taking back ten minutes of the day after the children are down. The ritual creates a container for something that is only yours: not earned, not scheduled, not shared.

practice.whenToUse: Use in the evening once the children are in bed or occupied. Good for nights when the whole day has been given to other people.

content.text: A short evening ritual for taking back ten minutes. The reclaim is not a reward for completing everything. It does not need to be earned. It is a small portion of the day that is yours by default.
New to rituals as a practice? Read
Wait until the children are in bed or occupied with something that does not need you.
Go somewhere in the house that is not primarily theirs. If there is no such room, sit on the edge of your bed with the door closed.
Choose one thing that is only for you: a cup of tea, a page of a book, a stretch, three songs. Nothing productive. Nothing that will be noticed or undone by morning.
Do that thing without explaining it to anyone. Ten minutes. Do not cut it short because someone might need you.
The reclaim does not need to be elaborate. It does not need to be the same thing each time. It needs to be ten minutes that do not belong to anyone else.
